blanked: Her ______ allowed her to save enough money to buy a new bike.

definition: the point at which a plan or project becomes real
blanked: After months of work, the garden project finally came to ______.

definition: the state of having no point because it will not succeed
blanked: He realized the ______ of arguing with someone who would not listen.

definition: to shock or excite someone into taking action
blanked: The inspiring speech helped ______ the students to start a recycling program.

definition: talking too much about things that do not matter
blanked: The ______ neighbor talked for an hour about the weather.

definition: relevant and linked to the idea being discussed
blanked: The student's question was ______ to the lesson about volcanoes.

definition: the quality of seeming very impressive but often not realistic
blanked: The ______ of his plans made everyone doubt they could actually work.

definition: given or done without good reason; not needed
blanked: The ______ use of difficult words made the essay hard to read.

definition: very serious and causing great pain or suffering
blanked: The ______ injury needed months of recovery and physical therapy.

definition: made up of many different kinds of things
blanked: The classroom was ______, with students from many different countries.

definition: excessive pride or self-confidence
blanked: His ______ led him to believe he could pass the test without studying.

definition: a person who challenges widely accepted ideas or traditions
blanked: The young scientist was an ______ who questioned old theories.

definition: an odd habit or way of acting
blanked: One ______ was that he always wore mismatched socks.

definition: public shame or disgrace
blanked: The athlete faced ______ after being caught cheating in the competition.

definition: light that makes something able to be seen or easy to see
blanked: The ______ from the lighthouse guided ships safely to shore.

definition: not able to be changed
blanked: The laws of gravity are ______ and apply everywhere on Earth.

definition: rude and not showing proper respect
blanked: The ______ remark about the teacher's hair got the student in trouble.

definition: something that encourages a process to happen faster
blanked: The science fair was the ______ for her choice to study biology.

definition: to ask someone for something in a forceful and persistent way
blanked: The child would ______ her parents for a new puppy every single day.

definition: to question or challenge the truth or honesty of something
blanked: The lawyer tried to ______ the witness by pointing out mistakes in the story.

definition: the glow produced by something very hot
blanked: The ______ of the campfire lit up the faces of all sitting around it.

definition: showing clear thinking and sharp insight
blanked: Her ______ questions during the debate impressed the judges.

definition: the state of not fitting together or being out of place
blanked: The ______ of a snowman on the beach made everyone laugh.

definition: to teach something by repeating it often
blanked: The coach tried to ______ good sportsmanship in every player on the team.

definition: to protect someone against loss or damage
blanked: The insurance policy would ______ the family if their house was damaged.

definition: being so poor that you struggle to afford daily meals and clothing
blanked: The charity worked to help families suffering from ______ in the town.

definition: laziness; avoidance of activity or work
blanked: His ______ during summer break meant none of his chores got done.

definition: moving forward steadily and impossible to slow down or stop once started
blanked: The ______ march of time means all grows older each day.

definition: to try to gain favor by flattering someone
blanked: The new student tried to ______ himself with the teacher by bringing her an apple.

definition: not harmful or hurting feelings
blanked: The ______ spider in the corner was completely harmless to humans.

definition: so boring and dull that it fails to hold your attention
blanked: The ______ movie had no exciting scenes, and half the audience fell asleep.

definition: a casual lack of concern about something
blanked: His ______ about the upcoming test worried his parents.

definition: a person who becomes involved without being asked to come
blanked: The cat was an ______ at the dog park and attracted curious stares.

definition: to insert something between other parts
blanked: The editor chose to ______ a new chapter between chapters three and four.

definition: a period between two rulers or governments
blanked: During the ______ the council managed the country until a new leader was chosen.

definition: refusal to change your own mind or compromise
blanked: The ______ of both sides made it impossible to reach an agreement.

definition: easily made angry
blanked: The ______ old man shouted at children who walked across his lawn.

definition: traveling from place to place for work
blanked: The ______ musician played in different towns every week.

definition: using very few words to express something
blanked: His ______ reply of just 'fine' did not tell us much about his day.

definition: a feeling of weariness and lack of energy
blanked: After a long hike in the hot sun, she felt ______ and wanted to nap.

definition: generous giving of money or gifts
blanked: The billionaire's ______ funded new libraries in five different cities.

definition: great generosity and forgiveness, especially toward a rival or enemy
blanked: The winner showed ______ by congratulating the losing team warmly.

definition: wrongful or illegal conduct by a person in power
blanked: The mayor was accused of ______ for using public money for personal trips.

definition: not truthful; lying
blanked: The ______ salesman made false promises about the product's quality.

definition: changing mood or behavior quickly and without warning
blanked: His ______ personality meant he could be happy one minute and sad the next.

definition: a system where people advance based on their abilities
blanked: In a ______, hard work and talent matter more than family connections.

definition: to calm someone down when they are angry or upset
blanked: The free dessert helped ______ the angry customer who had waited too long.

definition: great kindness in giving money or gifts
blanked: The school was built thanks to the ______ of a local business owner.

definition: just starting to develop
blanked: ______ technology like self-driving cars is still being tested and improved.

definition: very wicked or criminal
blanked: The villain's ______ plan to steal the diamonds was stopped by the detective.

definition: not direct or clear in meaning
blanked: She gave an ______ answer that did not really address the question.

definition: the small number that tells how many times to multiply
blanked: In 2³, the ______ 3 means multiply 2 times 2 times 2, which equals 8.

definition: the outer layer of an organ, especially the brain
blanked: The cerebral ______ is the wrinkly outer part of the brain that helps us think.

definition: an extra thing added that is not the main part
blanked: The after-school art club was an ______ to the regular class, giving students extra practice.
